t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Customer ID - platform-internal primary key. Assigned at registration. Unique within etoro DB. Used as the universal customer identifier across all tables.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Customer ID (RealCID). Hash distribution key. References Dim_Customer.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Drops 'platform-internal primary key', 'Assigned at registration', 'Unique within etoro DB', 'universal customer identifier' context; adds DWH-specific distribution note not from upstream

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: APEX broker external ID. Decimal(38,0) to accommodate APEX very large numeric ID format.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: APEX broker external ID. Decimal(38,0) to accommodate APEX very large numeric ID format.

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Ticker symbol. 'USD', 'EUR' for forex; 'AAPL.US', 'TSLA.US' for US stocks (format: TICKER.EXCHANGE); 'BTC' for crypto. Unique across all instruments. Use this for human-readable instrument identification.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Ticker symbol / ISO currency abbreviation. Resolved via Dim_Currency.Abbreviation on CurrencyID.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Introduces factually wrong term 'ISO currency abbreviation' — eToro tickers for stocks (e.g. AAPL.US) are NOT ISO currency codes; drops specific examples, exchange-format notation, and uniqueness claim

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Exchange rate from deposit currency to USD at processing time. Cannot be 0 in production.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Exchange rate from rollback tracking or Fact_BillingDeposit. Cannot be 0 in production.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Incorrectly claims source includes rollback tracking; SP source code shows ONLY BDEP.ExchangeRate (Fact_BillingDeposit). Also drops 'from deposit currency to USD at processing time.'

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Customer's assigned regulatory jurisdiction. DEFAULT 0. Sourced from Ext_FSC_BackOffice_RegulationChangeLog.ToRegulationID — end-of-day change. See §2.4. FK to Dim_Regulation.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Customer's regulatory jurisdiction from Fact_SnapshotCustomer at event date. 0=None, 1=CySEC, 2=FCA, 3=NFA, 4=ASIC, 5=BVI, etc. FK to Dim_Regulation.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Drops end-of-day change semantics from RegulationChangeLog; truncates enum (9 of 15 IDs missing: eToroUS, FinCEN, FinCEN+FINRA, FSA Seychelles, ASIC&GAML, FSRA, FINRAONLY, MAS, NYDFS+FINRA)

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Primary key identifying the loyalty tier. 1=Bronze, 2=Platinum, 3=Gold, 4=Internal (excluded), 5=Silver, 6=Platinum Plus, 7=Diamond. 0=N/A (DWH ETL placeholder). IDs are NOT in rank order -- use Sort for ordering. FK from Dim_Customer. Excludes Internal in customer-facing queries: WHERE PlayerLevelID <> 4.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: eToro Club loyalty tier from Fact_SnapshotCustomer. 1=Bronze, 2=Platinum, 3=Gold, 5=Silver, 6=Platinum Plus, 7=Diamond, 4=Internal (excluded). FK to Dim_PlayerLevel.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Drops 'IDs are NOT in rank order -- use Sort for ordering' (important sort gotcha); drops 0=N/A placeholder; drops 'WHERE PlayerLevelID <> 4' filter guidance

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Short code for the regulation. Used in V_Dim_Customer and analytics dashboards. Values match production Dictionary.Regulation.Name.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Regulation short code resolved via Dim_Regulation.Name.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Drops usage context ('Used in V_Dim_Customer') and production-match guarantee

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Reference exchange rate before fee markup. Fee spread = ExchangeRate - BaseExchangeRate. Added by Adi (19/09/2019).
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Reference exchange rate before fee markup. Fee spread = ExchangeRate - BaseExchangeRate.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Drops attribution metadata only; semantic meaning preserved

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Tier display name: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Platinum Plus, Diamond, Internal, N/A. Used in BackOffice reporting JOINs and customer-facing UI.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: eToro Club tier name. Resolved via Dim_PlayerLevel.Name on PlayerLevelID. 1=Bronze, 2=Platinum, 3=Gold, 5=Silver, 6=Platinum Plus, 7=Diamond.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Mixes in ID-to-name mapping from PlayerLevelID element (not from Name element); drops 'Internal, N/A' values; drops usage context; upstream Name element does not contain ID mappings

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Human-readable restriction state label. Unique per status. Used in BackOffice UI, compliance reports, and monitoring dashboards. Note: some values have trailing spaces in live data -- apply RTRIM() for string comparisons.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Account restriction state name. Resolved via Dim_PlayerStatus.Name on PlayerStatusID from Fact_SnapshotCustomer.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Drops critical operational gotcha about trailing spaces (RTRIM required for string comparisons); drops 'Unique per status'; drops BackOffice usage context

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Full country name in English. Unique per row. Used in UI dropdowns, compliance documents, and analytical reports.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Registration country name resolved via Dim_Country.Name on Fact_SnapshotCustomer.CountryID.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: 'Full country name in English' dropped; 'Unique per row' dropped; usage context dropped

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Full country name in English. Unique per row. Used in UI dropdowns, compliance documents, and analytical reports.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Country detected from customer IP at registration. Resolved via Dim_Country.Name on Dim_Customer.CountryIDByIP.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Same Dim_Country.Name pattern as RegCountry; 'Full country name in English' and 'Unique per row' dropped

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Card brand name. Unique constraint prevents duplicates in production. Used in payment UI, transaction records, and fraud reporting. Renamed from `Name` in production. 0=None, 1=Visa, 2=Master Card, 3=Diners, 4=Amex, 5=Fire Pay, 6=JCB, 7=American Express, 8=Maestro, 9=Laser, 10=Switch, 11=UK Local Credit Card, 12=Discover, 13=Local Card, 14=China Union Pay, 15=Solo, 16=Cirrus, 17=GE Capital.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Card network brand name. Resolved via Dim_CardType.CarTypeName on Fact_BillingDeposit.CardTypeIDAsInteger. Active: 1=Visa, 2=Master Card, 3=Diners, 8=Maestro.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Drops full 18-value enum (only 4 shown); drops unique-constraint warning; drops 'Renamed from Name in production'; 'Card brand name' → 'Card network brand name'

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Full country name in English. Unique per row. Used in UI dropdowns, compliance documents, and analytical reports.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Country of the card BIN resolved via Dim_Country.Name on Fact_BillingDeposit.BinCountryIDAsInteger.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Same Dim_Country.Name pattern; 'Full country name in English' and 'Unique per row' dropped

issues.problem: Wiki description says 'Ticker symbol / ISO currency abbreviation' but Dim_Currency.Abbreviation contains stock tickers like 'AAPL.US' and 'TSLA.US' — these are NOT ISO currency abbreviations. An analyst would incorrectly assume ISO codes and fail joins against currency lookup tables. Upstream wiki is explicit: 'Ticker symbol. AAPL.US, TSLA.US for US stocks (format: TICKER.EXCHANGE)'. The term 'ISO currency abbreviation' is factually wrong.

Drops the operational gotcha from Dim_PlayerStatus.Name: 'Note: some values have trailing spaces in live data -- apply RTRIM() for string comparisons.' An analyst filtering or joining on PlayerStatus string values without RTRIM would silently get wrong results.

Wiki says 'Exchange rate from rollback tracking or Fact_BillingDeposit.' SP source code shows ONLY BDEP.ExchangeRate (Fact_BillingDeposit) is used for ExchangeRate — rollback tracking table is used for ExchangeFee and rollback amounts but NOT for ExchangeRate. The lineage file has the same error. Misleads analysts about the source.

Tagged (Tier 2 — SP_Deposit_Reversals_PIPs) but SP does `dpl.Name AS [Label]` with explicit JOIN to Dim_PlayerLevel on PlayerLevelID. This is a dim-lookup passthrough — per tier rules must be (Tier 1 — Dictionary.PlayerLevel). The SP quirk (Label column contains PlayerLevel names, not Label names) should be documented in the description, not used to demote the tier.

Footer states '12 T1, 25 T2' (12+25=37) but actual Element table count is 14 Tier 1 and 23 Tier 2 (also 37). Review-needed sidecar lists 14 column names under 'Tier 1 (12 columns)' — an internal inconsistency in both files.

verdict.regeneration_feedback: Re-run with: (1) Fix Currency (row 12) — replace 'ISO currency abbreviation' with verbatim Dim_Currency.Abbreviation upstream: 'Ticker symbol. AAPL.US, TSLA.US for US stocks (format: TICKER.EXCHANGE); BTC for crypto. Unique across all instruments.' (2) Fix PlayerStatus (row 28) — append verbatim from Dim_PlayerStatus.Name: 'Note: some values have trailing spaces in live data -- apply RTRIM() for string comparisons.' (3) Fix ExchangeRate (row 13) — remove 'rollback tracking' as a source; per SP code ExchangeRate is ONLY from Fact_BillingDeposit.ExchangeRate (BDEP.ExchangeRate). Also fix lineage file. (4) Re-tag Label (row 19) as (Tier 1 — Dictionary.PlayerLevel); keep the SP-quirk note in the description. (5) Fix footer to 14 T1, 23 T2. (6) Re-quote RegCountry, RegCountryByIP, BinCountry from Dim_Country.Name verbatim: 'Full country name in English. Unique per row.' prefix each. (7) Add PlayerLevelID sort gotcha: 'IDs are NOT in rank order -- use Sort for ordering.' (8) Re-quote Club from Dim_PlayerLevel.Name (not from PlayerLevelID element): 'Tier display name: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Platinum Plus, Diamond, Internal, N/A.'
